description_long: This is a two-story wooden house with a prominent red gable roof featuring two dormer windows on the front side. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, and the house is elevated on stilts, creating a covered area beneath the main living space. A wooden staircase with railings leads up to a small landing and the main entrance, which is located on the side of the house. The roof includes skylights on one side, and the dormers add architectural interest and natural light to the upper floor. The structure appears to be designed for residential use, possibly in a suburban or semi-rural neighborhood.
description: A two-story wooden house with a red gable roof, dormer windows, and an elevated main floor accessed by an exterior staircase.
description_view_0: Front view showing two dormer windows, the main stilted structure, and the staircase leading to the upper level.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gable end, wooden siding, and the staircase with landing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the roof with two skylights and the plain wooden siding.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the entry door at the top of the staircase and the gable roof profile.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with dormer and skylight features
attribute_entryway_design: Side entry with wooden stairs, landing, and simple door; no porch or columns, minimal decorative elements.
